improvement: Added a dedicated GitHub Actions preflight that tests the executable OIDC runner, requests a scoped GitHub OIDC token, verifies its RS256 signature through pinned discovery metadata and JWKS, authorizes exact invocation claims, asserts source-SHA binding, and uploads only sanitized evidence.

verification.reason: The repository was not reachable through local DNS; the committed workflow was fetched back through the GitHub connector and inspected. No workflow dispatch tool was available in this run.

security_properties: checkout and artifact upload actions are pinned by full commit SHA
OIDC and request credentials are not persisted in the emitted artifact
acceptance is bound to repository, workflow, ref, environment, event, run, actor, and source commit
trust_limits: No live Pages hostname or served surface was verified.
A successful identity preflight would authenticate the workflow invocation, not scientific, medical, or deployment truth.
Replay rejection across separate workflow runs remains a separate control.
privacy_boundary: Contains only public repository paths, commit identifiers, bounded workflow metadata, and no private chat, health material, credentials, token values, diagnosis, or treatment claims.
